annotations.label: Kullback–Leibler divergence

anchor.snippet: Mathematically, it is defined as

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `klDiv μ ν` for arbitrary measures via the Radon–Nikodym derivative and log-likelihood ratio, generalizing the Wikipedia lead's formula.

annotations.label: Non-negativity of KL divergence

anchor.snippet: KL divergence is always a non-negative real number

annotations.note: Nonnegativity of `klDiv` follows from its `ℝ≥0∞`-valued definition, with the integral form proved nonnegative by `integral_llr_add_sub_measure_univ_nonneg`.

annotations.label: Relative entropy (discrete case)

anchor.snippet: For discrete probability distributions P and Q defined on the same sample space

annotations.note: Mathlib only provides the general measure-theoretic `klDiv`; no specialized discrete-sum formula `∑ p(x) log(p(x)/q(x))` is recorded.

annotations.label: Relative entropy (continuous case)

anchor.snippet: For distributions P and Q of a continuous random variable

annotations.note: Subsumed by the general measure-theoretic `klDiv`; no dedicated density-version integral `∫ p(x) log(p(x)/q(x)) dx` lemma is stated.

annotations.label: Relative entropy (general measure case)

anchor.snippet: More generally, if P and Q are probability measures on a measurable space

annotations.note: `InformationTheory.klDiv μ ν` is exactly the general measure-theoretic Kullback–Leibler divergence via the Radon–Nikodym derivative.

annotations.label: Gibbs' inequality (non-negativity)

anchor.snippet: Relative entropy is always non-negative

annotations.note: Explicitly labeled `**Gibbs' inequality**` in the source, this lemma proves nonnegativity of the integral form of `klDiv`.

annotations.label: Invariance under parameter transformations

anchor.snippet: Relative entropy remains well-defined for continuous distributions, and furthermore is invariant under parameter transformations

annotations.note: No `klDiv_map` or measurable-equivalence-invariance lemma for `klDiv` was found.

annotations.label: Additivity for independent distributions

anchor.snippet: Relative entropy is additive for independent distributions

annotations.note: Mathlib's chain rule for composition-products specializes to additivity for product (independent) measures, but no direct `klDiv (μ ⊗ ν) (μ' ⊗ ν')` decomposition is stated.

annotations.label: Convexity in the pair of probability measures

anchor.snippet: is convex in the pair of probability measures

annotations.note: No `convexOn`/joint convexity lemma for `klDiv` was found via grep or loogle.
